agent_analysis.hypothesis_rationale: Spending more on fundraising inputs (as measured by the proxy) directly helps secure larger contributions as campaigns are more effective at targeting donors.
agent_analysis.prior_art_notes: Thornton (2006) discusses the returns to fundraising inputs.
agent_analysis.interpretation: A highly statistically significant positive correlation was found. Nonprofits spending more on fundraising receive 14.3-fold returns in total contributions, indicating that fundraising investment is an effective mechanism for expanding philanthropic capital.
